items.title: State which definition you mean
items.avoid: Saying 'we need AI' without specifying broad field vs narrow product capability.
items.prefer: Open docs with 'AI (broad): …' or 'AI product (narrow): …' and link to ML or Gen AI siblings when relevant.
items.why: Shared vocabulary prevents engineers and stakeholders from talking past each other.
items.example: PRD: 'Narrow AI: automate ticket routing via supervised classifier' + link to Supervised topic.

items.title: Anchor claims in task boundaries
items.avoid: Marketing copy that implies human-level understanding.
items.prefer: List inputs, outputs, success metrics, and known failure modes for the specific task.
items.why: Narrow AI is auditable; vague 'intelligence' is not.
items.example: 'Classifies support tickets into 12 queues at 94% top-1 accuracy on last quarter's data.'

items.title: Defer depth to canonical sibling topics
items.avoid: Re-explaining ML training loops or Gen AI harms inside the AI definition topic.
items.prefer: One-sentence placement plus deep links to Machine Learning, Supervised, and genai-fundamentals topics.
items.why: Keeps the roadmap non-duplicative and maintains a single canonical owner per concept.
